text:
The Shadow (, Kage) was the Hunter Association's secret Special Operations squad, which carried out covert missions across several different countries. Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission https://www.vap.co.jp/hxh-movie/story/index.html 

Overview

Jed led the Shadow, the Hunter Association's black ops squad. They worked covertly under the association for a time before the team was dismantled by The Chairman for their usage of "On" - a cursed power fuelled by pure hate. The original members were destroyed, and though, for a time, their descendants were left to settle peacefully, it was eventually decided that all traces of the Shadow should be removed, and thus, the descendants were held in Kanan Prison Camp. Three escapees Gaki, Shura, and Rengoku would later use On to revive Jed and lead a charge against the Hunter Association at Heavens Arena in an attempt to force them into releasing the "Black Report". The team are eventually defeated by Gon, Killua, Kurapika, Leorio and Netero. 

History

Hunter Association

The Shadow was a group of agents that acted under the Hunter's Association, taking on missions and secret jobs that the Association didn't want to be made public. Headed by Jed, the self-appointed leader, the group began to make use of "On" - a power functioning as the opposite of Nen that is fuelled by a user's pure hatred. This use of power, deemed too dangerous and violent, prompted Chairman Isaac Netero to dismantle the organization. 

Jed and Netero are implied to have battled many times over the use of On versus the use of Nen, always leaving it as an amicable draw. Though at some stage, when it was decided all On users should be eliminated for their overly violent methods and crimes committed through the use of this power, it was Netero himself who carried out the mission, wiping out Jed's people. A fierce battle between the two ensued where, despite Netero's attempts to stop Jed from fighting against him, the On user called upon the ability Grudge of 100 Demons, having Rakshasa emerge to fight. Jed makes the vow that On will continue even after he has passed away, but he is countered by Netero's 100-Type Guanyin, Bodhisattva, who makes his own vow to eradicate On. Netero defeats Jed, and thus, the Shadow dies with him, finally losing its last original remaining member. Netero sealed away any record of the Shadow's existence in a file called The "Black Report". 

Descendants

After the destruction of the Shadow, the descendants of its original members were initially left to live peacefully and settle in a secluded village in the mountains. The nation eventually decided, and Garcia, a higher-up in either the government or Hunter Association itself, that any ties to the Shadow should no longer exist. The remaining descendants were persecuted, which led to a revolt, attracting unwanted attention. 

Garcia then decided it was best to remove the descendants of the Shadow entirely from the public eye, so he enlisted Hunters to capture the descendants and hold them in Kanan Prison Camp, which was described as harsh and depraved. Many of the descendants died while living in the prison camp; however, three of them, Gaki, Shura, and Rengoku escaped as children and fled, seeking out a way to revive On. 

Members

Original

Jed is the only member of the original Shadow to be named, although at least eighteen other members' silhouettes were seen. It is unknown exactly how many members there were in total. 

All original members of the Shadow are deceased. 

Descendants

Plot

The Last Mission

After its destruction at the hands of Netero, the Shadow as an organization ceases to exist. However, in a flashback, three descendants of Shadow members escape Kanan Prison Camp, seeking to revive On. In their search, they receive information from a mysterious figure that they may well be able to revive Jed himself through the power of an On contract. Using their hatred of the world they live in, they call upon Jed, and he appears before them, alive once more. They bow to him, introducing themselves as his most loyal followers, and swear their revenge against all hunters. Jed accepts their grudge and bestows power upon them through his blood, creating a restriction and Covenant with On. 

The group then traveled together with a plan to take revenge against the hunters who oppressed them and, in the present day, infiltrated the prison camp to recruit the other descendants into their cause. 

The group, led by Jed, arrives in the city as the Battle Olympia Tournament is about to begin. They encounter Leorio, having Gaki knock him out before advancing to take their place, hidden amongst the crowd of spectators. After Netero's announcement, the group split off to complete their separate tasks. Jed goes to the locker room, where the floor masters wait, anticipating their matches, and takes them under his control. Shura goes to the control room to shut off the elevators and prepare the building for lockdown, Rengoku makes her way to Netero's floor to seal off his Nen using her On ability, Onbasara and Gaki steps into the arena in place of Zushi's original opponent. 

Shura then locks down the building, and the prisoners, freed by the team, surround the Hunters and spectators with weapons. With Netero's Nen sealed by Rengoku, Jed arrives at Netero's floor, broadcasting The Chairman's predicament to the arena, where Gaki has just beaten Zushi. Seeing their friend hurt, Gon and Killua, who had been having tea with Netero, resolve to make Jed pay, only to be thrown out of the window by his immense power. Shura sends the floor masters, now under Jed's control, after Gon and Killua, who broke their way back into Heaven's arena. Meanwhile, the arena is still being held under the freed prisoners and the watchful eye of Gaki, with Jed broadcasting once more to tell them if anyone moves, everyone, including Netero, will die. 

Jed takes Rengoku and Netero to the roof of the building and then makes a call to the Hunter Association, explaining who they are and why they're here. He gives them an ultimatum that the Black Report, the file containing all of the information on the Shadow, be handed over by five pm, giving them ten hours to make the choice. Garcia is then brought up to the roof by one of Jed's soldiers and begins bargaining for his life. Jed tells him to beg Netero instead, but as he steps closer, he is strangled by Rengoku's On. Garcia recognizes the brand on her neck as that of a prisoner of Kanan Camp and admits he gave the order for the descendants of the Shadow to be locked up there. It is implied he got this order from Pariston. Jed explains that he wants this mistreatment, along with all of the Shadow's missions that the Hunter Association tried to cover up, to be made public to expose them and ruin their reputation. 

In the arena, Bisky recognizes Gaki's fighting style as On and asks if he is a descendant of the Shadow. He recalls that they were contained in a mountainous village. Gaki explains that despite living peacefully, their very existence riled the town. They were framed for plotting a coup, persecuted, and locked in Kanan Prison Camp, where many of them died under the harsh working conditions. He swears they will get revenge using the power of On that Jed bestowed upon them. 

The Hunter Association sends out tanks and blimps full of soldiers, all of whom are quickly wiped out by Jed and Gaki. Meanwhile, Shura watches Gon, Killua, and Kurapika's movements. In his boredom, Gaki requests to fight Gon and Killua. 

Gaki begins his battle with Gon and Killua, resolved by his memories of The Hunter Association's treatment of his people, while concurrently, Kurapika enters the control room containing Shura. They begin a battle of their own, during which Kurapika, much like Bisky, recognizes Shura's fighting style as a descendant of the Shadow and believes that despite their sad history, their revenge plan is unjustified. 

After losing his battle with Gon and Killua, Gaki expresses his respect for their strength and Zushi's. However, not long after, his On covenant, which was imposed by Jed, activates. Should he lose a battle to a Hunter, Gaki is set to self-destruct, and he urges Gon and Killua to get away from him before it sets off. Jed, who is still on the roof, seems to sense the demise and is saddened by it. 

Shura also eventually loses out in his battle with Kurapika, who is aided by Leorio after faltering in his fight, falling to the belief that he and Shura are one and the same in their quests for revenge. As he is dying, Shura launches a vial of Jed's blood and Leorio, but instead, Kurapika stands in its way and is injected. Shura tells him he can only survive by making a covenant with On and living in hatred and revenge. Kurapika refuses, and Shura dies, proclaiming in his final moments that he has fulfilled his duty to Jed. 

After noticing Shura's control of the weaponry has subsided, Bisky, Wing, and Zushi take the chance to attack Jed's soldiers who have been holding them there. Upon their success, they opt to evacuate the arena. 

Gon and Killua, in an attempt to save Kurapika, decide to take on Jed. Realizing they can't even land a hit, Gon makes a covenant with On to allow him to fight at Jed's level. Jed is pleased by this outcome as his goal for On to continue far beyond him is closer to realization with each person who takes up a covenant. Killua, seeing Gon in trouble, infiltrates Rengoku's on and appeals to her, talking about his fight with Gaki and how her friends wouldn't want her using this power. A tear rolls down her cheek as her On disengages, freeing Netero and ending her life. 

A battle then ensues between the remaining Jed and a newly freed Netero, all the while Killua is attempting to break Gon free of his On covenant. Netero refuses to fight, only blocking Jed's attacks instead of making his own. Eventually, Gon breaks free of On and, despite his protests, also purges Jed's body of On. As Jed's body disintegrates, he expresses no regret, saying his score with Netero is not settled yet. 

With this, the Shadow is officially eliminated, and Netero hopes that Jed can rest with peace in his heart. 

Trivia

Gaki is the only descendant of the Shadow not to be given a title or designation in the group.
